Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate both expressions for the given value.

Buying by the case Joe can buy his favorite ice tea at a convenience store for $$\text{$1.99}$$ per bottle. At the grocery store, he can buy a case of $$12$$ bottles for $$\text{$23.88} .$$ ⓐ Use the distributive property to find the cost of $$12$$ bottles bought individually at the convenience store. (Hint: notice that $$\text{$1.99}$$ is $$\text{$2} - \text{$0.01} .$$ ) ⓑ Is it a bargain to buy the iced tea at the grocery store by the case?

Multi-pack purchase Adele’s shampoo sells for $$\text{$3.97}$$ per bottle at the drug store. At the warehouse store, the same shampoo is sold as a $$\text{3-pack}$$ for $$\text{$10.49} .$$ ⓐ Show how you can use the distributive property to find the cost of $$3$$ bottles bought individually at the drug store. ⓑ How much would Adele save by buying the $$\text{3-pack}$$ at the warehouse store?
Simplify $$8 ( x - \frac{1}{4} )$$ using the distributive property and explain each step.
Explain how you can multiply $$4 ( \text{$5.97} )$$ without paper or a calculator by thinking of $$\text{$5.97}$$ as $$6 - 0.03$$ and then using the distributive property.
